subject: Re: Company Listing Request — Opal Screens
context: Ron runs MicroLED-Info directory (metalgrass.com). Asked who manufactures our display tiles and wants technical details on the microLEDs.
body:
Hi Ron,


Thanks for getting back to me. I'm glad to introduce you to the brand.


Opal Screens partners with established LED manufacturers in Shenzhen for our display modules, and we spec our own panel configurations and processing around their chip-on-board microLED packages.  Everything goes through final assembly and calibration happens state-side. Our current lineup uses flip-chip COB across all series, which is how we hit the tight pixel pitches (down to 0.7mm on Onyx).


A few technical highlights:


  • BlackFire anti-glare coating, present on all except Doublet. Matte nano-coating applied at the module level, eliminates reflection without sacrificing contrast.
  • NanoPix on the Onyx series. Our calibration and pixel-level uniformity correction applied on .7pp
  • SilkStream 240Hz on the Boulder series. This is our uniquely engineered solution allowing for panel refresh rates exceeding 15,000Hz allowing for full 240Hz video sources
  • Front-serviceable magnetic modules across all indoor series
  • Water series is full IP65 with conformal coating, rated 4,000+ nits

Happy to send over a full spec sheet for each series if that's helpful for the listing. Let me know what format works best for your directory.
attachments: Offer to attach spec sheet PDFs if approved

subject: Re: Product Introduction — Opal Screens MicroLED Video Walls
context: Dan is Group Director of Content at Emerald (Commercial Integrator, SecuritySales.com). Offered either a sponsored sidebar in the DVLED Deep Dive (May/June issue) with video Q&A and print ad, OR free press release coverage.
body:
Hi Dan,


Appreciate the detailed rundown on the DVLED Deep Dive — that sounds like a strong fit for us, especially with the timing. We're definitely interested in the sponsored sidebar option.

Could you connect us with your colleague to go over the specifics? We'd like to understand the pricing, deadlines, and what you'd need from us in terms of content and assets.


Looking forward to it.

subject: Re: Manufacturer Membership Inquiry — Opal Screens
context: CEDIA Expo sales director. Sent booth pricing (10x10 = $4,975, goes up from there) for Denver Sept 2-4. Jody Larsen from CEDIA membership made the intro. Need to decide if the cost is worth it for a first show.
body:
Hi Chris,


Thanks for the info and the introduction, Jody.


We're interested in exhibiting at CEDIA Expo this year, but quite honestly it might be something we need to push into 2027. Regardless, a 10x10 or 10x20 would be our likely starting point. A couple of questions before we move forward:


  • What's the CEDIA member discount on booth pricing?
  • What's the deadline to lock in space for September?
  • Are there any preferred booth locations still open in the display-related section of the floor?  Unfortunately, the link to view the open spaces wasn't working on our end.
  • What does the booth package include — just the space, or is there any shell/furniture included?

We'd also want to bring a working MicroLED display wall, so we'd need to confirm power availability and any rigging restrictions for the booth.


Let us know next steps and we'll get the ball rolling.

subject: Quick follow-up — correct email for Opal Screens
context: Follow-up to establish info@opalscreens.com as the correct contact. Previous email accidentally sent from grant@gamma.tech.
body:
Hi Ron,


Quick follow-up — my previous email came through from our parent company's address by mistake. For anything Opal Screens related, this is the right inbox to use going forward: info@opalscreens.com.


Everything in the previous message still stands. Let me know if you'd like those spec sheets.
